caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is 5-digit proxy and residual is broad.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Certification is evidence about refrigerant tasks, not every plumbing task.
inputs.pi_dist: Residential versus commercial revenue mix varies materially.
inputs.pi_moat: Licensing supports demand but is not itself a pricing moat.
inputs.t50_years: Vendor-sponsored survey; AI agent use is not the same as effective firm-wide automation.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Commercial MEP survey rather than exact NAICS census.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not an observed NAICS owner-age statistic.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a census of 238220 and consolidators are used only for B/M context.
inputs.buy_mult: BizBuySell reports SDE/owner earnings, not normalized EBITDA; larger targets can price higher.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct small-platform HVAC EBITDA exit series was reopened.
inputs.pricing_structure: Exact price-book share is not sourced.

Validator: ServiceTitan's vendor-sponsored commercial MEP survey supports the 29% AI-agent statistic and listed sales/CRM use cases, but it does not directly evidence NAICS-wide effective adoption, flat-rate or membership pricing prevalence, or the selected capture fractions.

Validator: BizBuySell supports small-business SDE benchmarks and Capstone supports broad construction-services M&A/public comparators, but neither directly supports 12 active NAICS 238220 consolidators, a 3.0x normalized-EBITDA entry, or a 7.0x small-platform exit; the borderline aggregate is sensitive to these proxy estimates.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.0796 (margins.json[23]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Engineering/Construction EBITDA/Sales 7.96%); revenue/employee $260,272 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 238220); class <5: 63,588 firms, avg revenue/firm $386,166 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $30,739 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 20,175 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,348,756 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $107,361 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 12,459 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,960,335 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $235,643 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 9,415 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,892,743 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $787,462 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 1,152 firms, avg revenue/firm $49,865,618 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,969,303 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 215 firms, avg revenue/firm $311,824,335 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $24,821,217 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,152

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI exposure is concentrated in office, sales support and reporting; physical service, installation and supervised field work remain human tasks.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 23822 ($100,763,014,046) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 23822 ($306,277,104,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: ancestor 23822. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

Validator: ServiceTitan's vendor-sponsored commercial MEP survey supports the 29% AI-agent statistic and listed sales/CRM use cases, but it does not directly evidence NAICS-wide effective adoption, flat-rate or membership pricing prevalence, or the selected capture fractions.

Validator: BizBuySell supports small-business SDE benchmarks and Capstone supports broad construction-services M&A/public comparators, but neither directly supports 12 active NAICS 238220 consolidators, a 3.0x normalized-EBITDA entry, or a 7.0x small-platform exit; the borderline aggregate is sensitive to these proxy estimates.
source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. All mechanics, exact-artifact bindings, deterministic equality, role/dataset injection, and arithmetic pass. All four registered sources are accessible and support their core stated facts; S2-S4 only partially support the broader score bridges. The durable physical-service thesis remains useful, while adoption/capture scope and transaction-multiple proxies require visible qualification.

sources.title: Section 608 Technician Certification | US EPA
sources.what_it_supports: EPA says covered refrigeration and air-conditioning technicians must be certified and pass an EPA-approved test.

sources.title: ServiceTitan Commercial Sales and Marketing Report
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 survey of over 1,000 primarily mechanical/electrical/plumbing commercial leaders: 29% used AI agents; current uses include content, lead qualification and CRM updates.

sources.title: HVAC Business Valuation Multiples & Financial Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: Reported 2021–25 sold HVAC businesses averaged 2.75x owner earnings; sold-business earnings multiple quartiles were 1.99x, 2.58x, 2.75x and 3.33x.

sources.title: Capstone Partners Construction Services M&A Coverage Report, August 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Public construction-services index showed 18.9x average LTM EBITDA; it is a public-company comparator, not small HVAC deal evidence.

title: Plumbing, Heating, and Air-Conditioning Contractors
